placements.reasoning: Placed near hand/contact-action cluster including sacudir, pellizcar, apretar, and señalar. Empujar is a body action verb and fits naturally beside other force/manipulation actions.
Placed adjacent to postural/body-position verbs sentarse, acuclillarse, doblar, and arrodillarse. Agacharse is semantically another crouching/bending posture action.
Placed beside descansar, estornudar, dormir, and flotar, where involuntary/body-state actions already appear. Bostezar aligns closely with sleep/rest and respiratory reflex actions.
Placed between posture and object-handling actions, near aventar and atrapar. Cargar is a general handling/carrying body action and fits this manipulation zone.
Placed near estornudar, salpicar, and rociar in a cluster of mouth/air-output and spray-like actions. Chiflar is also produced with breath and mouth movement.
Used a replaceable cell and placed next to bañarse and near descansar/dormir as part of a daily-body-state cluster. Despertar pairs especially well with dormir.
Placed next to apretar, señalar, and reventar in the force/manipulation area. Jalar is the counterpart to empujar, so keeping them nearby preserves semantic organization.
Placed beside morder and near sangrar, grouping mouth-related body actions together. Masticar is strongly anchored to morder.
Used a replaceable cell in the top row body-care/body-state area near bañarse and masajear. Rascarse is another self-directed body action and fits that cluster.
Placed near estornudar, bostezar, and chiflar to create a respiratory/breath-action cluster. Respirar is the core body-breath action in that region.
Placed near patear, deslizar, and the throw/catch area above, extending the object-control cluster. Sostener belongs with handling and controlling objects with the body.
Placed near deslizar and close to bodily reaction actions such as sudar and lastimarse above. Temblar is a body-state/reaction verb and fits the right-side reaction cluster.
Placed beside respiratory and mouth-output actions respirar and chiflar, with estornudar directly above. Toser belongs in this same breath/reflex cluster.
Placed between motor-action and mouth-action areas, close enough to morder/masticar while preserving nearby movement clusters. Tragar is semantically tied to eating-mouth actions.
Used its own replaceable former location, which is ideal for preserving prior layout expectations. It also stays near brincar, escalar, and other large-movement actions.
Used a replaceable cell and placed near bañarse, masajear, and presionar in a touch/contact self-care cluster. Frotar fits naturally with rubbing and massage-like actions.
Placed on the far right next to balancear and below salir, preserving the directional movement/locomotion edge of the grid. Entrar is the natural semantic counterpart to salir.
Placed adjacent to dormir in a sleep/rest cluster. Acostarse is highly related to lying down and bedtime routines.
Placed near respiratory/reflex actions toser, respirar, estornudar, and bostezar. Hipo functions as a body reflex and fits this body-reaction cluster.
Used a replaceable cell formerly associated with brincar, in an activity/motion area near driblar, jugar boliche, and patear. Botar fits as a bouncing motion action in that sports-movement cluster.

conflicts.description: The provided occupied-state lists (row=0, col=1) as bañarse, while REPLACEABLE CELLS also says (row=0, col=1) was a hidden/inactive word. Under the inactive-exclusion rule, hidden items are not active occupants, so the active grid item bañarse was treated as immovable and the replaceable note was ignored for placement.
conflicts.resolution: Did not use (0,1) because it is occupied by the active visible item bañarse; used other valid replaceable/empty cells instead.
